name: data-processing
description: Process JSON with jq and YAML/TOML with yq. Filter, transform, query structured data efficiently. Triggers on: parse JSON, extract from YAML, query config, Docker Compose, K8s manifests, GitHub Actions workflows, package.json, filter data.
qualityReason: Specific CLI tools for config processing
capabilities: Extract fields from JSON and YAML files using jq and yq syntax
Filter arrays and objects with select conditions
Query Docker Compose, Kubernetes, and GitHub Actions files
Convert between TOML, YAML, and JSON formats
author: 0xDarkMatter
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing educational examples for jq and yq CLI tools. All files are markdown documentation with example patterns - no executable code, no network calls, no file system access, and no external commands. The bash code blocks are static documentation examples showing syntax, not actual code to execute.
valueStatement: Developers need to extract, filter, and transform data from configuration files and API responses. This skill provides ready-to-use patterns for jq and yq so you can quickly query and modify structured data without memorizing syntax.
userTitle: Process JSON YAML and TOML data efficiently
limitations: Requires jq and yq CLI tools to be installed separately
Does not execute data processing commands directly
Cannot process binary or unstructured data formats
Limited to command-line tool patterns shown in documentation

useCases.title: Query Kubernetes manifests
useCases.description: Extract container images, labels, and configurations from K8s deployment files for audit and documentation.

useCases.title: Inspect package.json
useCases.description: Quickly view dependencies, scripts, and configuration from package.json without opening the file in an editor.

useCases.title: Process API responses
useCases.description: Filter and extract specific fields from JSON API responses for testing and debugging workflows.

promptTemplates.title: Basic JSON extraction
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I extract the version field from a JSON file using jq?

promptTemplates.title: Filter array items
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to filter JSON array items where status equals active using jq select.

promptTemplates.title: Query Docker Compose
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I list all container images from docker-compose.yml using yq?

promptTemplates.title: Modify YAML config
promptTemplates.prompt: Update a YAML configuration file with a new version number using yq in-place edit.

bestPractices: Use jq -r for raw output when you need unquoted strings without quotes
Chain filters with pipes to progressively transform data
Test filters with jq '.' first to verify structure before applying transformations
antiPatterns: Using eval or exec with user input to construct jq queries - risk of injection
Processing untrusted JSON without validating structure first
Modifying files without creating backups when using in-place edits

faq.question: What operating systems support jq and yq?
faq.answer: jq and yq work on macOS, Linux, and Windows via WSL. Install with brew on macOS or apt/yum on Linux.

faq.question: What are the resource limits for large JSON files?
faq.answer: Performance depends on available memory. Files under 100MB process instantly. Very large files may require streaming with jq.

faq.question: How does this integrate with other skills?
faq.answer: Use with file reading skills to first load config files, then apply jq or yq patterns for extraction and transformation.

faq.question: Is my data safe when processing files?
faq.answer: This skill only provides patterns. The actual jq and yq tools run locally. No data is sent to external servers.

faq.question: Why are my jq queries failing?
faq.answer: Common causes include incorrect field paths, missing quotes, or malformed JSON. Verify file structure first with jq '.'

faq.question: How does this compare to Python JSON libraries?
faq.answer: jq and yq are faster for quick command-line tasks. Python offers more flexibility for complex transformations and debugging.
